title: Competitive Intelligence Review
summary: A monthly cadence for monitoring competitor moves, market shifts, and emerging threats. Stay informed without becoming obsessed.

steps.title: Scan competitor updates
steps.description: Visit the websites and social channels of your top 5 competitors. Check for: pricing changes, new features or products launched, new positioning or messaging, new partnerships or integrations, new content themes (signals their strategy).
steps.tool: Browser, Google Alerts, Crayon or Klue if available

steps.title: Review win/loss intelligence
steps.description: Check your last month's sales data: which deals did you win against a specific competitor? Which did you lose? Ask lost-deal prospects: why did you choose the alternative? This is the most valuable competitive intelligence you can get.

steps.title: Update competitive matrix
steps.description: Update your competitive analysis spreadsheet with any changes from the scan. Note: new pricing, new features, new positioning. Highlight anything that changes the competitive landscape meaningfully.

steps.title: Assess strategic implications
steps.description: For each significant competitive move, ask: Does this threaten our position? Does it create an opportunity? Do we need to respond? If yes, what is the minimum effective response? Most competitive moves require no response — do not overreact.

steps.title: Decide on responses (if any)
steps.description: If a competitive move requires a response, define: what will we do, by when, and who is responsible? Add it to your quarterly priority check. Most months, the answer is 'no response needed — stay the course.'

kpis.target: Completed within first week of each month
All significant moves logged within 2 weeks of occurrence
Feedback from at least 3 lost deals per quarter

whenToImplement: Once you have identified your top 5 competitors and are actively selling against them. Not necessary in the pre-product phase.
delegationNotes: The scanning and matrix updates are the first things to delegate — to a VA, analyst, or ops person. The strategic interpretation and response decisions remain with the founder. Set up Google Alerts as a low-effort starting point.
automationNotes: Google Alerts for competitor names and key industry terms. Social monitoring tools (Mention, Brand24) for real-time competitive chatter. Some CRM platforms have competitive intelligence features built in. The goal is to automate data gathering so the monthly review focuses on thinking, not searching.
